skill.name: avalonia-zafiro-development
skill.description: Guidance for building Avalonia UI applications with Zafiro, ReactiveUI, DynamicData, functional error handling, and strict MVVM boundaries.
skill.summary: Apply Zafiro conventions and reactive MVVM patterns to Avalonia application development.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only markdown prose, C# code examples, and inline code spans. No executable scripts, network access, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are inline markdown code spans and fenced C# examples that document Avalonia, Zafiro, ReactiveUI, and DynamicData APIs. They do not invoke a shell, execute Ruby backticks, or provide executable installation scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside markdown formatting and C# documentation examples. I found no script file, shell invocation, Ruby execution context, or instructions to run these snippets as commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Ordinary Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to normal documentation phrases and headings, including method design, stable APIs, guide descriptions, and table labels. No cryptographic primitive, hash function, cipher, key handling, or security implementation appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain software design prose and markdown table headings, not cryptographic code. The surrounding files are development guidelines without executable crypto-related behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Matched UI and Design Terms
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings reference Avalonia VisualStates, static state guidance, and a Zafiro-first principle. These are architecture rules, not host inspection, environment discovery, or data collection behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is UI state, coding standards, and toolkit preference. I found no commands or instructions that enumerate the host, filesystem, network, users, or environment.

content.user_title: Build Avalonia Apps with Zafiro Patterns
content.value_statement: Avalonia teams often drift into inconsistent reactive patterns and UI-coupled ViewModels. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete Zafiro conventions for maintainable MVVM code.
actual_capabilities: Guides Avalonia code toward strict MVVM boundaries and UI-independent ViewModels.
Recommends DynamicData operators for collection pipelines and binding-ready observable collections.
Promotes Zafiro helpers for validation, lifecycle management, commands, and reactive shortcuts.
Defines naming and error-handling conventions for C# projects using CSharpFunctionalExtensions.
Provides reusable patterns for refreshable collections, validation rules, and user-facing error handling.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts, generators, or automated project scaffolding.
Assumes the project already uses Avalonia, Zafiro, ReactiveUI, and DynamicData.
Does not replace framework documentation or compatibility checks for package versions.
Contains conventions only, so final architecture choices still need human review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Reactive ViewModels
use_cases.description: Apply consistent DynamicData, ReactiveUI, and Zafiro patterns when reviewing or writing Avalonia ViewModels.

use_cases.title: Review UI Architecture
use_cases.description: Check whether proposed code keeps Avalonia types out of ViewModels and follows clean architecture boundaries.

use_cases.title: Refactor Collection Workflows
use_cases.description: Replace ad-hoc observable collection logic with documented RefreshableCollection and validation patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Basic Conventions
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small Avalonia change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Avalonia ViewModel change using the avalonia-zafiro-development skill. Focus on MVVM boundaries, naming, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Reactive Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this reactive collection workflow to follow Zafiro and DynamicData conventions. Explain which operators or helpers should replace the current approach.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Zafiro Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the ViewModel and reactive data flow for this Avalonia feature using Zafiro-first patterns, validation rules, and functional result handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Architecture Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Avalonia module for violations of the skill rules. Identify UI coupling, ad-hoc sources, subscription misuse, and missing Zafiro abstractions.

output_examples.input: Review a ViewModel that directly references Avalonia brushes and manually updates a list after loading data.
output_examples.output: The review flags UI coupling in the ViewModel and recommends moving Avalonia brush selection to the view layer. It also suggests a RefreshableCollection-style flow for loading and updating items.

output_examples.input: Refactor validation logic that checks collection state inside a subscription.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a Zafiro validation rule driven by a DynamicData pipeline. It explains why validation should remain declarative and lifecycle-managed.

output_examples.input: Plan error handling for a command that loads projects from a service.
output_examples.output: The answer proposes functional result handling for expected failures and a user notification pipeline for command errors. It reserves exceptions for unrecoverable conditions.

best_practices: Search for existing Zafiro helpers before adding custom reactive utilities.
Keep ViewModels independent from Avalonia UI types and platform-specific concerns.
Use DynamicData collection operators before plain reactive operators for list workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not put business logic inside Subscribe handlers.
Do not create ad-hoc SourceList or SourceCache instances for local one-off problems.
Do not use exceptions for expected domain or validation flow.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding tools apply Avalonia, Zafiro, DynamicData, ReactiveUI, and MVVM conventions consistently.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete Avalonia application?
faq.answer: No. It provides development guidance and patterns, not a runnable scaffold or project generator.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found markdown guidance and C# examples only, with no executable or malicious behavior.

faq.question: Does it require Zafiro knowledge?
faq.answer: Yes. It assumes the user wants Zafiro-first conventions and can adapt them to the project.

faq.question: Can it review existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide reviews for MVVM boundaries, reactive pipelines, validation, lifecycle management, and error handling.
